active_ingredients: Pantoprazole sodium 20 mg and 40 mg delayed-release tablets; powder for oral suspension; IV form (prescription only)
indications_and_usage: Indicated for short-term treatment of erosive esophagitis associated with GERD and to maintain healing of erosive esophagitis; also for pathological hypersecretory conditions including Zollinger-Ellison syndrome. A proton pump inhibitor; often chosen for patients on clopidogrel because it has the least effect on clopidogrel activation among PPIs. Not available over the counter in the US.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to pantoprazole or other substituted benzimidazoles; do not use with rilpivirine-containing products.
warnings_and_cautions: Acute interstitial nephritis; Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea; bone fractures with long-term, high-dose use; cutaneous and systemic lupus erythematosus; cyanocobalamin (B12) deficiency and hypomagnesemia with prolonged use; possible masking of gastric malignancy symptoms - investigate alarm features.
adverse_reactions: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain, vomiting, flatulence, dizziness, arthralgia; in children - upper respiratory infection, fever, rash.
drug_interactions: Reduced absorption of acid-dependent drugs (ketoconazole, itraconazole, atazanavir, nilotinib, erlotinib, iron salts, mycophenolate); may increase methotrexate levels; warfarin (monitor INR); affects atazanavir/nelfinavir; contraindicated with rilpivirine; least clinically significant interaction with clopidogrel among the PPIs.
dosage_and_administration: Adults: erosive esophagitis - 40 mg once daily for up to 8 weeks (an additional 8 weeks if not healed); maintenance - 40 mg once daily. Hypersecretory conditions - 40 mg twice daily, titrated. Children 5 years and older (>=15 kg): 20-40 mg once daily for up to 8 weeks for erosive esophagitis. Swallow tablets whole; take 30 minutes before a meal; exactly as prescribed.
overdosage: Experience with overdose is limited; symptoms have been consistent with known adverse effects; supportive care, not removed by dialysis. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
